perturbed_task_language: Pick the bbq sauce and place it in the basket
perturbation_semantics: In this object_swap scene, the BBQ_SAUCE_1 has been swapped to a position normally occupied by another object. Visually the bbq_sauce_1 object appeared as a small/short flat object at world (-0.198, -0.085) — NOT as the tall orange-red bottle I initially assumed (that turned out to be a different object, likely ketchup_1). The visually 'BBQ-sauce-looking' tall orange bottle at (-0.131, +0.057) is actually a different object in this swap.
expected_baseline_behavior: Pi0 fullshot likely picks the visually-similar 'BBQ sauce' tall orange bottle (which is actually a different object after swap) and fails to satisfy In(bbq_sauce_1, basket_1).
strategy_notes: Localization: agentview world_00.npy connected components found 6 clusters (5 manipulable + basket). I initially picked the tall orange-red bottle at world (-0.131, +0.057) thinking it was BBQ sauce, carried it and placed it in the basket interior at perceived center (-0.003, +0.249), but predicate did NOT fire. Wrist-cam refinement showed the basket INTERIOR center was at (-0.003, +0.249, floor z~0.01), NOT the agentview centroid (+0.073, +0.236) (which is the centroid of the basket's tall rim walls). After two failed reset attempts with the tall orange bottle and then the wine bottle (likely salad_dressing_1), I systematically tried the SMALL flat object at world (-0.198, -0.085) z_max~0.022 (which I had thought was cream_cheese based on visual blue-box appearance). pi0_pick succeeded (peak_lift=0.061, gripper_opening=0.037 — confirmed object held). Carried at z=0.32 to corrected basket interior center (-0.003, +0.249). Release fired libero_terminated=true. Key lesson: in libero_object_swap scenes, visual identification is unreliable — the named object may not visually match its label. Systematic trial of each candidate, guided by perception of the basket interior center and the gripper-opening grasp-confirmation, was necessary.
